title: ForgeBrain Analytics: Performance Snapshot and Strategy Performance Aggregate
description: Not active in Phase 1 — see analytics-spec.md Section 1. Defines the two shapes Analytics produces: a per-reel PerformanceSnapshot (feeds back into memory/memory-schema.json's audience_response) and a cross-topic StrategyPerformanceAggregate (feeds the strategy_performance extension points already reserved in brain/content-director-spec.md, brain/script-spec.md, and brain/storyboard-spec.md).

snapshot_id.description: Unique identifier for this performance snapshot.

based_on_publishing_package_id.description: The package_id of the PublishingPackage this reel was posted from.

computed_performance_score.description: Normalized 0-1 score derived from metrics, written into memory.topics[topic_id].performance_score. Derivation formula is intentionally not fixed here — see analytics-spec.md Section 6.

computed_retention_score.description: Normalized 0-1 score derived from metrics.average_retention_percent, written into memory.topics[topic_id].retention_score.

snapshot_taken_at.description: When these metrics were captured. A topic may accumulate multiple snapshots over time as metrics mature (e.g. day 1, day 7, day 30).

audienceResponse.description: Field names and types deliberately match memory-schema.json's audienceResponse definition exactly, since this is what gets written into it.

aggregate_id.description: Unique identifier for this aggregate, e.g. a stable hash of dimension+value.

dimension.description: Which strategy field (from content-director-schema.json or storyboard-output-schema.json) this aggregate groups by.

value.description: The specific value within that dimension being aggregated, e.g. 'myth' for dimension: hook_type.

sample_size.description: How many reels contributed to this aggregate. Small sample sizes should be treated with proportionally less confidence — see analytics-spec.md Section 6.

contributing_topic_ids.description: Which topics' reels fed this aggregate, for traceability back to individual PerformanceSnapshots.
